title: Epitaph for Solanos the Syrian
support: A marble plaque, damaged on the right and below, and broken vertically down the middle in two, but subsequently reconnected. The text appears to be complete.

material._: Fine-grained, banded, grey marble, likely Aphrodisian, Proconnesian, Hymettan, Pentelic, Lunense, Ephesian_scritto. Digital microscopy: homeoblastic to heteroblastic fabric (identification based on pXRF, digital microscopy)

layout.p: The text fills the available space, appearing therefore to have been engraved after the damaged to the right and lower edges.

handNote.lettering: Lunate epsilon and sigma, cursive alpha and mu.

provenanceFound._: Catacomb of S.Giovanni, now on display in Sector F of the Siracusa museum.

museum.name: Museo Archeologico Regionale Paolo Orsi
museum.description: An outstanding collection of archaeological material. The collection ranges from Siracusa and the surrounding territory (Megara Hyblaia, Akrai, Kasmenai etc) to encompass all of eastern Sicily, reaching as far as Gela and Agrigento. The ground floor covers prehistory through to the end of the Classical period. Upstairs includes Hellenistic and Roman Syracuse and a display of material from the catacombs. A gallery displaying material from Hellenistic eastern Sicily is in preparation.
